title: Compat API: unify pull/push and add missing progress info
body:
Progress bar in JSONMessage is missing compared to docker output both in
pull and push. Additionaly, pull was not using JSONMessage while push
was using this type.
[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

Signed-off-by: Jakub Guzik <jguzik@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Compat REST API: image push and pull have now progress bars
```

comments.body: @vrothberg I noticed that too, exploring testing. It's related to some fields not being filled properly in JSON messages structs. I'll look later for an answer.
/hold

Thanks!

I also noticed that `remote push` is not printing anything but that predates this PR.

> Thanks!
> 
> I also noticed that `remote push` is not printing anything but that predates this PR.

Do you refer to `podman-remote`?
Yes, `podman-remote`.
I opened https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14971 to track it.

I know. It's not related since it's not compat API and I think it needs https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14886 solving first as the fix is actually the same.

The difference is that `podman-remote push` has nothing in the endpoint implementation (just return status, write message) while `podman remote-pull` has at least a simple `stream` forward (current status, on the `main` branch).

> I opened #14971 to track it.

@vrothberg I think you opened a duplicate of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11554. https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11554 just misses a statement that there is absolutely no progress, but if you try to solve https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11554 you see this right away.

> > I opened #14971 to track it.
> 
> @vrothberg I think you opened a duplicate of #11554. #11554 just misses a statement that there is absolutely no progress, but if you try to solve #11554 you see this right away.

I don't think so. https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11554 is asking for progress bars. https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14971 states that there is not output at all which should be there.
The issue of duplicate outputs predates this PR.  Just tried on a fresh install.  Apologies for not noticing before.
@vrothberg, hmm I think you were right speaking at least about pull. I tested today the newest version I pushed (this one) and do not see duplicates at first glance.

I just checked, at least the current version is working as expected, there are no duplicates (bigger image downloaded):
```
$ DOCKER_HOST=unix:///run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock docker pull mongo
Using default tag: latest
593c62d03532: Download complete 
d7bfe07ed847: Download complete 
20cec14c8f9e: Download complete 
97ef66a8492a: Download complete 
38c3018eb09a: Download complete 
ccc9e1c2556b: Download complete 
be887b845d3f: Download complete 
e5543880b183: Download complete 
1a103a446c3f: Download complete 
c8b57c4bf7e3: Download complete 
docker.io/library/mongo:latest
```
Do you have the rights to retest by the way? Locally tests pass.

> Do you have the rights to retest by the way? Locally tests pass.

I do and restarted a number of times but it hits the timeout.  Maybe you can repush and then we'll see what's going on.
I know where the duplicates come from now.  Needs some fix in c/image.
The following PR addresses the duplicates: https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1615

> > Do you have the rights to retest by the way? Locally tests pass.
> 
> I do and restarted a number of times but it hits the timeout. Maybe you can repush and then we'll see what's going on.

Ok, I'll investigate it later, thanks.
Tests aren't hip yet though

> Tests aren't hip yet though

I don't see how the changes could cause a timeout.  Could you rebase another time and repush?

> > Tests aren't hip yet though
> 
> I don't see how the changes could cause a timeout. Could you rebase another time and repush?

@vrothberg Done, it seems they work this time. 
If finally merged that would be my 50th commit to podman (yes, I like stats :slightly_smiling_face: ) So I guess, :tada:
/hold cancel

Congrats, @jmguzik!  And thanks for contributing.

@Luap99 PTAL
/assign @Luap99
